title: Why Most People Ignore Their Psychic Hits
meta_description: You have already experienced psychic hits. You just ignored them. This article explains why most people dismiss their intuition and how to stop. Learn to recognize and trust your natural abilities.
og_title: Why Most People Ignore Their Psychic Hits
og_description: You have already experienced psychic hits. You just ignored them. This article explains why most people dismiss their intuition and how to stop. Learn to recognize and trust your natural abilities.
body:
You've Already Had The Hits. You Just Didn't Trust Them.

Let me guess. You've walked into a room and known who was about to call before your phone even rang. You've thought about an old friend for no reason, and then they texted you an hour later. You've had a bad feeling about a decision, ignored it, and then watched everything fall apart.

And then you told yourself it was a coincidence. Right?!

Here's what nobody told you: those moments aren't flukes. They're not random. They're your psychic hits - signals from your intuitive system that you're picking up on information outside the five physical senses. And most people spend their whole lives ignoring them, dismissing them, or explaining them away.

Why? Because we've been trained to. From the time you were a kid, you were taught that the only real information comes through your eyes, ears, and logic. The gut feeling? The sudden knowing? The shiver down your spine when something's off? That got labeled as "imagination" or "anxiety" or "being too sensitive."

But here's the truth that changes everything: your sensitivity is not a flaw. It's a sense. And like any sense, it gets stronger the more you use it, and it gets duller the more you ignore it.

So let's talk about why you've been ignoring your hits, and more importantly, how to start trusting them without losing your mind.

"The psychic hit is not a loud voice. It's a whisper you've been trained to ignore. The work isn't learning to hear it - it's learning to stop pretending you didn't."

The Real Reason You Dismiss Your Intuition

It's not because you're not psychic. You are. Everyone is. The difference between someone who seems "gifted" and someone who's still confused is simple: the gifted person stopped arguing with themselves.

When a hit comes through - that flash of knowing - the average person immediately starts a mental debate:

  • "That's just my imagination."
  • "I'm probably overthinking this."
  • "There's no way I could know that."
  • "I'm just being paranoid."
  • "What if I'm wrong? I'll look stupid."

And in that split second of doubt, the signal gets buried. You talk yourself out of the truth before you even have a chance to verify it. Know what I mean?

This isn't your fault. It's conditioning. You live in a world that worships the five senses and treats anything beyond them with suspicion. Your nervous system is calibrated for a wider range - you feel more, sense more, know more - and that makes other people uncomfortable. So they taught you to shut it down.

But here's the thing: your nervous system doesn't stop picking up information just because you've been told to ignore it. It keeps sending the signals. You just stop listening.

The Cost of Ignoring Your Hits

When you dismiss your psychic hits, you're not just losing party tricks. You're losing guidance. Your intuition is your internal GPS - it's designed to keep you safe, aligned, and moving toward what's right for you.

Every time you ignore a hit:

  • You walk into situations that felt wrong from the start.
  • You stay in relationships you knew were off.
  • You take jobs that drain you.
  • You miss opportunities that were literally knocking.

And the worst part? You start to doubt yourself so deeply that you stop even noticing the hits at all. Your system goes quiet. Not because it stopped working, but because you stopped acknowledging it.

That's the bright wound - the pain of knowing more than you allow yourself to act on. It's the ache of being sensitive in a world that rewards numbness. And it's the exact place where your psychic development has to begin.

The Five Most Common Ways You Sabotage Your Own Psychic Hits

Let's get specific. Here are the patterns I see over and over in people who have strong sensitivity but can't seem to trust it. See if any of these sound familiar.

1. You Wait For Proof Before You Act

Your intuition doesn't come with a receipt. It doesn't hand you a spreadsheet of evidence. It gives you a feeling, a knowing, a nudge. And you want to wait until you have three sources of confirmation before you move.

But here's the thing: by the time you have proof, the moment has passed. The hit was meant to guide you before the information was obvious. That's the whole point.

I had a client who kept getting a hit to call her sister on Tuesday afternoons. She ignored it for weeks because she didn't have a "reason." When she finally called, her sister was in the middle of a crisis and desperately needed someone to talk to. The hit was the reason. She just didn't trust it.

2. You Mistake Anxiety For Intuition (And Vice Versa)

This is the big one. Anxiety and intuition can feel almost identical in the body - both create a sense of urgency, both can make your stomach clench, both can speed up your heart. But they're coming from completely different places.

Anxiety is fear-based. It loops. It repeats the same worried thought over and over. It feels frantic and scattered.

Intuition is calm. Even when the message is urgent, it has a stillness to it. It arrives as a single clear knowing, not a spiral of what-ifs.

If you're constantly second-guessing whether you're anxious or intuitive, start paying attention to the quality of the feeling. Does it feel tight and repetitive? That's anxiety. Does it feel clear and settled, even if it's uncomfortable? That's intuition.

3. You Compare Yourself To "Real" Psychics

You see people online who claim to have visions, hear voices, see auras in full color. And you think, "Well, I don't have that, so I must not be psychic."

That's like saying you're not athletic because you can't run a marathon tomorrow. Psychic ability comes in flavors. Some people get images. Some get feelings. Some get words. Some just know things without any sensory input at all.

Your hits are valid in whatever form they show up. The person who gets a clear image and the person who gets a vague sense of "don't go there" are both receiving real information. Stop comparing your channel to someone else's.

4. You Dismiss The Small Stuff

You think a psychic hit has to be dramatic - predicting a death, knowing the winning lottery numbers, reading someone's mind perfectly. So when you get a small nudge like "take the other route to work" or "call this person today," you ignore it because it seems trivial.

But here's the truth: your intuition builds trust through small wins. When you follow the small hits and they prove accurate, your system gets stronger. You start to believe yourself. And then the bigger hits have a foundation to land on.

If you want to develop your psychic ability, start with the tiny things. Follow the impulse to move your car seat. Act on the thought to send a text. Notice what happens.

5. You're Afraid Of Being Wrong

This is the deepest one. You're terrified that if you trust your intuition and it's wrong, you'll look foolish. You'll lose credibility. You'll prove that you're not special after all.

So you stay safe. You stay in the gray zone of "maybe that was a hit, maybe it wasn't." You never commit to a reading, a decision, or a direction because you'd rather be uncertain than wrong.

But here's what I've learned after years of teaching this work: being wrong is part of the process. Every psychic I know has been wrong. Multiple times. The difference is they didn't stop. They adjusted, learned, and kept going.

Your accuracy will never improve if you're not willing to be wrong sometimes. That's just how learning works.

How To Start Trusting Your Psychic Hits Today

You don't need years of training. You don't need a guru or a certification. You need to start paying attention and stop arguing with yourself. Here's exactly how to do that.

Step 1: Create A "Hit Log"

Get a notebook or open a note on your phone. Every time you get a psychic hit - a sudden knowing, a strong feeling, a random thought about someone - write it down. Include the date, the hit itself, and what happened next.

Don't judge yourself. Don't analyze. Just record. Over time, you'll start to see patterns. You'll notice which hits were accurate and which were noise. And you'll build a body of evidence that your intuition is real.

This is the single most powerful thing you can do. It takes five minutes a day and it will change everything.

Step 2: Practice On Low-Stakes Decisions

Don't start with life-or-death choices. Start with things that don't matter much. Which checkout line should you stand in? What should you eat for lunch? Which route should you take to the store?

Before you make the decision, pause and ask your body: "What feels right?" Then follow that. See what happens. You'll be amazed at how often the hit is correct.

This builds the trust muscle. And once you trust yourself on the small stuff, the big stuff gets easier.

Step 3: Stop Explaining Everything

When you get a hit, your brain immediately tries to explain it away. "That's just because I saw a similar thing on TV." "That's just because I'm tired." "That's just because I'm anxious."

Your job is to stop the explanation before it starts. Instead of rationalizing, just say: "I don't know why I know this, but I do." And leave it there.

You don't need to prove the hit to anyone. Not even to yourself. You just need to acknowledge it.

What To Do When The Hit Feels Uncomfortable

Sometimes the hits aren't pleasant. You get a sense that someone isn't safe. You feel a warning about a situation. You know something bad is coming.

This is where most people freeze. They don't want to believe the uncomfortable hit because it's scary to think they might be right. So they ignore it.

But here's the thing: your intuition doesn't warn you to scare you. It warns you to prepare you. The information is a gift, even when it's hard.

When you get an uncomfortable hit:

  • Don't panic. Panic clouds the signal.
  • Ask for more information. "Show me what I need to know about this."
  • Take one small action. If the hit says "don't go to that event," don't go. You don't need to know why.
  • Trust that the "why" will reveal itself in time.

Your intuition is not trying to hurt you. It's trying to help you. The discomfort is just the gap between what you know and what you're ready to accept.

The Books That Helped Me Stop Ignoring My Own Hits

I didn't learn this stuff in a classroom. I learned it through practice, mistakes, and a few books that cut through the noise. If you're serious about developing your psychic sensitivity, these are the ones I recommend starting with.

"The Psychic Pathway" by Sonia Choquette is the book that first showed me that psychic ability is not a special gift for a few - it's a natural human capacity that we've been taught to suppress. Choquette writes with warmth and directness, and she gives you actual exercises to do, not just theory. If you've ever felt like you're "too sensitive" for this world, this book will feel like coming home. See on Amazon (paid link)

"You Are a Psychic" by Debra Katz takes a more scientific approach. Katz is a clinical psychologist who spent decades researching psychic phenomena, and she breaks down exactly how intuitive information comes through the body. This book helped me understand why my physical sensations were connected to my psychic hits - and how to tell the difference between a gut feeling and indigestion. See on Amazon (paid link)

"The Gift of Fear" by Gavin de Becker is not a psychic development book in the traditional sense, but it might be the most important book you'll ever read about trusting your intuition. De Becker is a security expert who teaches people how to recognize and act on the signals of danger that their bodies are already sending. Reading this book will make you stop dismissing your "bad feelings" forever. See on Amazon (paid link)

"The Intuitive Way" by Penney Peirce is the book I recommend for people who want a structured, step-by-step approach to developing their sensitivity. Peirce walks you through the stages of opening up - from noticing your first hits to making intuitive decisions your default mode. It's practical, grounded, and full of exercises that actually work. See on Amazon (paid link)

The One Question That Changes Everything

Here's the question I want you to sit with. Write it down. Put it somewhere you'll see it every day.

"What would I do if I actually trusted myself?"

Not what would you do if you had proof. Not what would you do if you were guaranteed to be right. What would you do if you simply trusted the knowing that's already inside you?

That question is the doorway. The answer is your next step.

You don't need to become someone else. You don't need to force anything. You just need to stop pretending you don't know what you already know.

The hits have been there all along. You've just been ignoring them. And that's okay - because now you know why, and now you know what to do about it.

Start small. Start today. And see what happens when you finally listen.

If you want to go deeper, I've put together a free assessment that will show you exactly where your psychic sensitivity is strongest and what's blocking you from trusting it. Take the assessment here - it takes about ten minutes, and it will give you a personalized roadmap for your next steps.
